title: rosa sepium f. agrestis
intro: Rosa sepium f. agrestis, also known as wild dog rose, is a shrub that belongs to the family Rosaceae and is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The medicinal properties of Rosa sepium f. agrestis are primarily derived from its flowers, which contain flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils. The health benefits of this plant include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, reduce stress, and boost the immune system. Rosa sepium f. agrestis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its flowers can also be used in potpourri and perfumes.
